title: Isoloba elatior
intro: Isoloba elatior is a plant species belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae), native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Isoloba elatior include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies in the form of dried roots and leaves.
taxonomy: Isoloba elatior is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the genus Isoloba, which is a part of the larger Gesneriaceae family. The classification of Isoloba elatior places it in the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is known for its unique characteristics.
